_meta.what: HELD KEY for the structural-reveal pressure. The measure (scripts/structural-reveal.mjs) never reads this. Only the scorer (scripts/structural-reveal-score.mjs) does.
_meta.claim: A line that bonds two standing entities sitting in SEPARATE regions of the entity graph is a structural reveal. The interior's connectivity channel (bridgeSurprise) fires maximally on it (component collapse) and ~0 on surface-matched controls; the mass-based one-surprise (bayesBits) does NOT isolate it (it ranks fresh-entity lines above the reveal). The connectivity reading is invariant to a surface emphasis cue.
_meta.mechanism: connectivity collapse over the CON/SIG bond graph, endpoints resolved through the causal SYN-merge identity quotient (src/core/bridge.js)

bridge.reveal: high (>= 0.5) — endpoints were in different components
bridge.adjacent_rebond: ~0 (<= 0.2) — endpoints already adjacent, a re-bond confirms
bridge.fresh_entity: ~0 (<= 0.2) — a brand-new entity is new mass, not a bridge

bayes.why: the reveal bonds two STANDING entities (tiny mass deposit); at least one fresh-mass line outranks it on bayesBits — the gap this pressure targets

invariance.why: bridge reads the bond structure, not the surface cue; the marked condition changes only an emphasis adverb
